title: PM Modi's June 2026 Europe Visit: France and Slovakia Diplomatic Tour Amid G7 Participation
lede: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will undertake a multi-nation European visit from June 14-18, 2026, covering France and Slovakia while participating in the G7 Summit, according to an official MEA briefing conducted on June 12, 2026.

sections.context: The Prime Minister's visit spans four days across three destinations in France and Slovakia, with participation in the G7 Summit as a central component of the itinerary. The Ministry of External Affairs organized a special briefing involving senior officials including Secretary (West) Shri Sibi George, Additional Secretary (North Europe) Smt Pooja Kapoor, Additional Secretary (Europe West) Shri Piyush Srivastava, and Joint Secretary (Multi Economic Relations) Shri Shambhu Hakki. This high-level preparation by multiple specialized departments underscores the diplomatic significance India places on the engagement. The visit represents both bilateral diplomatic outreach and India's participation in the premier multilateral economic forum of advanced democracies.
sections.threads: This European swing follows India's established pattern of deepening ties with major Western powers, particularly France, with which India has developed progressively deeper strategic partnerships over recent years. The inclusion of Slovakia in the itinerary reflects India's broader engagement strategy across Central and Eastern European nations. India's participation in the G7 as a guest demonstrates New Delhi's growing role in shaping discussions on global economic and strategic challenges, positioning India at the table of advanced economies on issues of international significance. [[india-fr-prime-minister-modi-to-visit-france-and-slovakia-partic-2026|The detailed itinerary and bilateral dimensions have been separately announced]], capturing India's multifaceted diplomatic approach.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor the substantive outcomes from bilateral meetings during the France and Slovakia legs of the visit, which will indicate the depth and trajectory of India's European partnerships. The nature of India's engagement at the G7 Summit—particularly on economic policy, geopolitical issues, and multilateral coordination—will signal how India intends to leverage its growing international standing. Further developments in [[india-fr-modis-three-nation-tour-france-elevates-india-to-specia-2026|India-France strategic partnership elevations]] should be tracked, as France has consistently been a priority partner for India in Europe. The outcomes from this June 2026 visit will set the tone for Indo-European diplomatic engagement over the subsequent quarters.
